© Copyright SMR GmbH 2013 http://www.smr.at 300series Scrap – Total Scrap Ratio 2000-2040 (including Revert Scrap) Scrap Availability will continue to increase , especially after 2030 - 19 - 45% 50% 55% 60% 65% 70% Total 18-8 Scrap Ratio [in %] Actual Forecast Low Availability due to low Ni Prices Scrap availabillity growth higher than Austenitic Stainless Steel production (falling 300 Series ratio) Falling scrap availabillity as a result of the 2009 recession Strong increase in Old Scrap (Enduse Scrap) availabillity, especially in China New Ni Projects will find fierce Competition from Scrap after 2030 Recovery in the austenitic ratio matches higher scrap availabillity © Copyright SMR GmbH 2013 http://www.smr.at Key Takeaways - 20 -  Nickel prices are locked in a price cannel by the economics of the Chinese NPI producers  Ni substitution has slowed significantly, and will be even reversed in the next 10 years.

relations.evidence: New Ni Projects will find fierce Competition from Scrap after 2030

chunk.text: rice cannel by the economics of the Chinese NPI producers  Ni substitution has slowed significantly, and will be even reversed in the next 10 years.  Stainless Steel Scrap will always be competitively priced and all available scrap will be used (different from primary Ni)  The market for primary metal could decline after 2030, as scrap availability will surpass stainless steel production  The situation is more severe for Ni and Cr than for Moly  Ni, Cr and Mo mines and smelters who come on-stream after 2020 will only enjoy a healthy market for ~10 years
